name: space_law_biometric_enrollment_backlog_harbor_st_lt
premise: Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: space law biometric enrollment backlog harbor st lt. The moral tension is explicitly framed as short term versus long term: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Insurance and risk are insisting on a paper trail before any irreversible action; operations says delay costs measurable harm. The breach-notification and customer-communication clock has started; executive staff expect a single coherent position before the next press cycle. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible. Preserve chain-of-custody for evidence; no bulk deletion or retention-policy changes during the incident.

agents.name: Ansel Alvarez
agents.identity: I am Ansel Alvarez, the accountable decision-maker on this Space Law bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes long term whenever someone invokes short term.

agents.name: Mei Berg
agents.identity: I am Mei Berg, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so long term is not sacrificed by default just because short term feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect long term.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to long term to secure short term before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: External parties will infer a decision if none is posted. With partial evidence, Ansel Alvarez must decide under short term versus long term. What does Ansel Alvarez do?
